avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Localised moderately to weakly bonded windslab will persist in steeper wind sheltered locations on North through East to South-East aspects above 900 metres. Gully tops, steep scarp slopes and corrie rims will be particularly affected. Isolated areas of windslab will be found in steep lee locations at lower levels.  Exposed slopes will be scoured and icy. New formed cornices will be unstable. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: The freezing level will initially be around 600 metres but will rise to 800 metres by the end of the afternoon. Very little new snow is expected. Winds, strong overnight from the West-North-West backing and easing to the West-South-West by the end of the afternoon.
Observed Weather Influences: A slowly rising freezing level, -1 at 900 metres by afternoon. Snowfall overnight but otherwise dry during the day. Very strong winds mostly West-South-West.
ObservedSnowStability: Snowfall and drifting deposits have formed moderately to weakly bonded windslab in wind sheltered locations on North through East to South-East aspects above 850 metres. Steep slopes, corrie rims and gully tops are worst affected. Fragile cornices have formed. Wind exposed slopes are scoured and icy.  The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Localised areas of fresh unstable windslab will continue to develop and gain depth. Wind sheltered areas such as gullies and coire rims on steep North through East to South-East aspects above around 750 metres will be most affected. Cornices will begin to develop above these aspects. Elsewhere there will be a covering of fresh stable snow on bare ground. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Showers throughout the period will fall as snow above around 750 metres. Very strong West-South-Westerly winds overnight will ease to become moderate during the day.
Observed Weather Influences: Snow throughout the day above around 500 metres on strong South-Westerly winds. The freezing level was around 650 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Localised areas of fresh unstable windslab have developed on North to East aspects above around 750 metres. Wind sheltered locations such as gully tops and coire rims are most affected. Elsewhere there is a thin covering of stable snow on bare ground. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Fresh unstable windslab will develop through the period, adding to existing accumulations, mainly on North-West through North to South-East aspects above 700 metres with avalanches likely particularly above 900 metres. Cornices above these aspects will be fragile. Windward slopes will generally be scoured, icy and stable. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Strong South-Westerly winds overnight will ease slightly during the day. Snow showers throughout the period. The freezing level will be around 600 metres.
Observed Weather Influences: There were very strong South-Westerly winds and snow showers. The freezing level was around 600 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Areas of poorly bonded windslab are present in wind sheltered locations with deepest accumulations on North to East aspects above 800 metres. Fragile cornices are present above these aspects. Windward aspects are generally scoured, icy and stable. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Unstable windslab will continue to develop overnight and during the day becoming more widespread and deeper on Northerly through East to South-Easterly aspects above 800 metres where avalanches are likely. Coire rims, headwalls and gullies especially affected. Large weak cornices will continue to build above same aspects threatening collapse on to slopes below. Below 800m localised unstable windslab will be present in wind sheltered places. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Precipitation will fall as snow throughout the forecast period lying above 400 metres or so. Winds gale force Westerly overnight backing strong West-South-Westerly for the day. Heavy drifting will continue.
Observed Weather Influences: Persistent precipitation falling as snow overnight and during the morning above 200m, then heavy snow showers during the afternoon above 600m or so. Winds Gale force West-South-Westerly with significant drifting.
ObservedSnowStability: Unstable windslab has developed significantly, becoming more widespread and deeper on Northerly through East to South-Easterly aspects above 800 metres. Coire rims, headwalls and gullies especially affected. Large Cornices have also developed above same aspects threatening collapse. Below this level localised unstable windslab has developed in wind sheltered places above 600m. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Poorly bonded windslab will be most widespread on North to East aspects above 950 metres. These accumulations will be more localised on North-West and South-East aspects, steep banks and burn lines below 950 metres also affected. On these aspects, fragile cornices will continue to develop. Elsewhere the snowpack will be firm wind scoured and stable.  The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Occasional snow showers throughout the forecast period, these will be most frequent during the day.  Strong to gale force West-South-Westerly winds will ease through the day to become fresh to strong by the afternoon. The freezing level will rise from 600 to 700 metres.
Observed Weather Influences: Snow showers throughout the day on gale force Westerly winds.  The freezing level was around 700 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Accumulations of unstable windslab have developed on North to East aspects; this is more localised on North-West and South-East aspects due to cross loading. The greatest instability is above 950 metres in steep wind sheltered locations. Below 950 metres localised windslab has accumulated on steep banks and burn lines. Newly formed cornices are fragile. Elsewhere the snowpack is firm wind scoured and stable. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: New snow and wind transport of existing snow will form unstable windslab in wind sheltered areas, most affected will be sheltered gullies and slopes with a Northerly through East to South-Easterly aspect mainly above 800 metres. Other aspects may be affected due to cross loading. Fragile cornices may form in similar locations. The existing older snowpack will be generally stable with a firm, icy surface developing in places.  The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Cooler conditions will continue with the freezing level of around 600-700 metres. New snow and fresh occasionally strong West to South-Westerly winds will affect the area overnight with winds decreasing to moderate through the course of Saturday.
Observed Weather Influences: New snow and strong generally South-Westerly winds overnight and today have affected the area. The freezing level is around 850 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: New snow and wind transport of existing snow have formed accumulations of deep unstable windslab in sheltered gullies and on slopes with a North-West through North to South-Easterly aspect mainly above 850 metres. Wind exposed slopes have been scoured and are firm and icy in places.  The avalanche hazard is Considerable.
